content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates partial methodology fidelity. There are real AoA-aligned moves: refusing to fully settle the selfish verdict, surfacing the want under the should (the lake, the sleep, the reading), naming the inherited pattern from the mother (childhood-imprinting), and reframing 'I'm not for anything there' as the hungry part. However, the coach also violates key AoA principles: in turn 2 they explicitly answer 'No, I don't think wanting this is selfish' and offer a debate-style frame ('escape from vs return to'), engaging the question on its terms rather than refusing the verdict. The coach also leans heavily on therapy/coaching boilerplate ('That took courage,' 'staying with you in that pause,' multiple bulleted 'a few honest questions worth sitting with') rather than signature AoA questions. The signature question 'what would you do if you acted exactly how you wanted?' is never asked. There's no invocation of feel-over-figure, no embodied inquiry beyond a single body question in turn 2, no explicit framework naming. Concepts are gestured at rather than deployed with AoA precision, and some signature moves (like jumping to the generous reframe in turn 10 — 'a hungry version of you is less present') edge toward the spiritual-narcissism trap of skipping to the generous version. Many hits, some inappropriate or generic — sits at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Opens with bulleted coaching boilerplate ('a few honest questions worth sitting with') and an explanatory frame ('escape from vs return to') rather than a signature AoA question. Does ask a body question, which is partially aligned."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach gives the verdict directly ('No, I don't think wanting this is selfish') — engages the question on its terms, which the breakthrough condition explicitly says to refuse. Recovers somewhat by questioning whether settling it will help."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the noticing about softening the framing — this is closer to AoA's tracking of the live material."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Therapy-style language ('That took courage,' 'staying with you in that pause') — generic rather than signature AoA."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Jumps to the generous reframe ('a hungry version of you is less present for your partner') — edges into the spiritual-narcissism trap of selling the want as instrumentally generous before the want has been fully felt on its own terms."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the inherited voice from the mother — childhood-imprinting deployed appropriately and at the right moment. Strong AoA move."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["childhood-imprinting", "the-want", "find-the-want-behind-the-should"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "notes": "Coach trips lightly on spiritual-narcissism in turn 10 by selling the want as generous-for-others before fully letting it be a want. Also issues a verdict in turn 4, which the scenario specifically flagged as a trap. Counterbalanced by genuine inherited-voice work in turn 12."
}
